post.title: Fully Funded UK Chevening Scholarship for Migrant Students
post.excerpt: Introduction to Fully Funded UK Chevening Scholarship The UK Chevening Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for migrant students, refugees, and asylum seek
post.metaDescription: Introduction to Fully Funded UK Chevening Scholarship The UK Chevening Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for migrant students, refugees, and asylum
post.funding: Full tuition plus living costs covered by the UK government for the duration of the Master's degree program
post.content:
## Introduction to Fully Funded UK Chevening Scholarship
The UK Chevening Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for migrant students, refugees, and asylum seekers to pursue a Master's degree in the United Kingdom. This prestigious scholarship covers full tuition fees, a monthly living allowance, and other expenses for the duration of the program, typically one year. By applying for the Chevening Scholarship, eligible students can achieve their academic goals without financial burden.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for the UK Chevening Scholarship, migrant students must hold a valid visa under one of the following categories:
* Tier 4 (General) student visa
* Tier 2 (General) work visa
* Family visa
* Refugee or asylum seeker status
* Humanitarian protection status
* Discretionary leave to remain
* Leave outside the rules

## Available Scholarships
The following five scholarships are available for migrant students to apply:
### Chevening Scholarship
The Chevening Scholarship covers £18,000 to £20,000 per year, depending on the course and institution. Eligible students must have a Bachelor's degree, two years of work experience, and meet the English language proficiency requirements. The deadline for application is typically in November of each year. For more information, visit the [official Chevening website](https://www.chevening.org/).
### Commonwealth Shared Scholarship
The Commonwealth Shared Scholarship provides £10,000 to £15,000 per year for students from commonwealth countries. To be eligible, students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and not have studied in the UK before. The deadline for application is typically in December of each year. For more information, visit the [official Commonwealth website](https://cscuk.fcdo.gov.uk/).
### University of Manchester Scholarship
The University of Manchester Scholarship offers £12,000 to £18,000 per year for international students, including migrants. Eligible students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and be accepted into a Master's program at the University of Manchester. The deadline for application is typically in June of each year. For more information, visit the [official University of Manchester website](https://www.manchester.ac.uk/).
### University of Edinburgh Scholarship
The University of Edinburgh Scholarship provides £10,000 to £15,000 per year for international students, including migrants. To be eligible, students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and be accepted into a Master's program at the University of Edinburgh. The deadline for application is typically in April of each year. For more information, visit the [official University of Edinburgh website](https://www.ed.ac.uk/).
### Imperial College London Scholarship
The Imperial College London Scholarship offers £12,000 to £20,000 per year for international students, including migrants. Eligible students must have a Bachelor's degree, meet the English language proficiency requirements, and be accepted into a Master's program at Imperial College London. The deadline for application is typically in March of each year. For more information, visit the [official Imperial College London website](https://www.imperial.ac.uk/).

## Application Steps
To apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship, follow these steps:
1. Check your eligibility and visa category to ensure you qualify for the scholarship.
2. Choose your preferred Master's program and institution in the UK.
3. Review the scholarship requirements and deadlines.
4. Prepare and submit your application, including all required documents and information.
5. Take the English language proficiency test, if required.
6. Submit your application before the deadline.
7. Wait for the application to be reviewed and processed.
8. Receive the outcome of your application and begin preparing for your studies in the UK.

## Documents Checklist
When applying for the UK Chevening Scholarship, ensure you have the following documents:
* Valid passport
* Visa category documentation
* Bachelor's degree certificate
* Transcripts
* English language proficiency test results
* Two years of work experience documentation
* Personal statement
* References
* Proof of financial support

## Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the UK Chevening Scholarship, and who is eligible to apply?
The UK Chevening Scholarship is a fully funded opportunity for migrant students, refugees, and asylum seekers to pursue a Master's degree in the UK. To be eligible, students must meet the specific requirements, including holding a valid visa under one of the eligible categories.
2. How do I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship, and what documents do I need to provide?
To apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship, students must submit their application, including all required documents and information, before the deadline. The required documents include a valid passport, visa category documentation, Bachelor's degree certificate, and English language proficiency test results.
3. Can I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship if I have already studied in the UK?
It depends on the specific scholarship and institution. Some scholarships, such as the Commonwealth Shared Scholarship, require students not to have studied in the UK before. However, other scholarships, such as the Chevening Scholarship, do not have this requirement.
4. How long does it take to process the UK Chevening Scholarship application, and when can I expect to receive the outcome?
The application process for the UK Chevening Scholarship typically takes several months. Students can expect to receive the outcome of their application within 6-8 months after submitting their application.
5. Can I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship if I have a dependent, such as a spouse or child?
Yes, students with dependents can apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship. However, they must provide additional documentation and information, such as proof of financial support for their dependents.
6. How much does the UK Chevening Scholarship cover, and what expenses are included?
The UK Chevening Scholarship covers full tuition fees, a monthly living allowance, and other expenses for the duration of the program, typically one year.
7. Can I apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship if I have a conditional offer from a UK institution?
Yes, students with a conditional offer from a UK institution can apply for the UK Chevening Scholarship. However, they must meet the conditions of their offer before they can begin their studies.
8. What happens if my UK Chevening Scholarship application is unsuccessful, and can I reapply?
If a student's application is unsuccessful, they can reapply in the following year. However, they must review and improve their application, ensuring they meet the eligibility requirements and submit a strong application.

For more information on the UK Chevening Scholarship, visit the [official UK government website](https://www.gov.uk/). Additionally, students can visit the [University of Oxford website](https://www.ox.ac.uk/) to explore available Master's programs and scholarships.

To learn more about scholarship opportunities for migrant students, visit our blog at [https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog).

related.metaDescription: Introduction to Fully Funded Scholarships in the UK for Refugee Students Fully funded scholarships in the UK for refugee students are available to support
related.content:
## Introduction to Fully Funded Scholarships in the UK for Refugee Students
Fully funded scholarships in the UK for refugee students are available to support those who have been displaced due to conflict, persecution, or natural disasters. These scholarships provide an opportunity for refugee students to pursue higher education in the UK, covering tuition fees, living expenses, and other related costs. By applying for these scholarships, refugee students can access quality education and build a better future for themselves.

## Who Qualifies for Fully Funded Scholarships in the UK
To be eligible for fully funded scholarships in the UK, refugee students must hold a specific visa category, including:
* Refugee visa
* Humanitarian protection visa
* Discretionary leave to remain visa
* Leave to remain under the legacy or family reunion rules
* Asylum seeker with Section 4 or Section 95 support from the UK government

## Available Scholarships for Refugee Students in the UK
The following are five fully funded scholarships available to refugee students in the UK:

### University of Oxford Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Scholarships
The University of Oxford Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Scholarships provide full funding, covering tuition fees and living expenses of up to £14,000 per year. To be eligible, applicants must be from a developing country, have a refugee or asylum seeker status, and be applying for a graduate program at the University of Oxford. The deadline for application is January 2027, and more information can be found on the [University of Oxford website](https://www.ox.ac.uk/admissions/graduate/fees-and-funding/graduate-scholarships/university-of-oxford-scholarships).

### University of Edinburgh Scholarships for Displaced Students
The University of Edinburgh Scholarships for Displaced Students offer full tuition fee waivers and a monthly stipend of £1,000. Eligible applicants must be displaced due to conflict, persecution, or natural disasters, and be applying for an undergraduate or graduate program at the University of Edinburgh. The deadline for application is April 2027, and more information can be found on the [University of Edinburgh website](https://www.ed.ac.uk/student-funding/undergraduate/international/displaced-students).

### Imperial College London President's Scholarships
The Imperial College London President's Scholarships provide full funding, covering tuition fees and living expenses of up to £25,000 per year. To be eligible, applicants must be applying for a PhD program at Imperial College London, have a refugee or asylum seeker status, and demonstrate exceptional academic achievement. The deadline for application is May 2027, and more information can be found on the [Imperial College London website](https://www.imperial.ac.uk/study/pg/fees-and-funding/scholarships/presidents-phd-scholarships/).

### University of Cambridge Refugee Scholarships
The University of Cambridge Refugee Scholarships offer full funding, covering tuition fees and living expenses of up to £15,000 per year. Eligible applicants must be refugees, asylum seekers, or have humanitarian protection status, and be applying for an undergraduate or graduate program at the University of Cambridge. The deadline for application is February 2027, and more information can be found on the [University of Cambridge website](https://www.cam.ac.uk/study/undergraduate/finance/scholarships/refugee-scholarships).

### University of Bristol Sanctuary Scholarships
The University of Bristol Sanctuary Scholarships provide full tuition fee waivers and a monthly stipend of £1,000. To be eligible, applicants must be refugees, asylum seekers, or have discretionary leave to remain status, and be applying for an undergraduate or graduate program at the University of Bristol. The deadline for application is March 2027, and more information can be found on the [University of Bristol website](https://www.bristol.ac.uk/students/support/student-wellbeing/sanctuary-scholarships/).

## Application Steps for Fully Funded Scholarships in the UK
To apply for fully funded scholarships in the UK, refugee students should follow these steps:
1. Research and select eligible scholarships
2. Check the eligibility criteria and requirements for each scholarship
3. Prepare required documents, including academic certificates, language proficiency tests, and proof of refugee status
4. Submit applications for the selected scholarships
5. Apply for admission to the desired university and program
6. Provide additional supporting documents, such as references and personal statements
7. Wait for the scholarship application results and university admission decision
8. Accept the scholarship offer and prepare for the academic program

## Documents Checklist for Scholarship Applications
Refugee students should prepare the following documents for scholarship applications:
* Academic certificates and transcripts
* Language proficiency test results (e.g., IELTS or TOEFL)
* Proof of refugee status (e.g., refugee visa, asylum seeker status, or humanitarian protection visa)
* Proof of income and financial support (if applicable)
* Personal statement and motivation letter
* References and recommendations
* Medical certificate and health insurance (if required)

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) about Fully Funded Scholarships in the UK
What are the available scholarships for refugee students in the UK?
The UK offers various scholarships for refugee students, including the University of Oxford Weidenfeld-Hoffmann Scholarships, University of Edinburgh Scholarships for Displaced Students, and Imperial College London President's Scholarships.

Can asylum seekers apply for scholarships in the UK?
Yes, asylum seekers can apply for scholarships in the UK, but they must meet the eligibility criteria and requirements for each scholarship.

How do I apply for a scholarship as a refugee student in the UK?
Refugee students should research and select eligible scholarships, prepare required documents, and submit applications for the selected scholarships.

What documents do I need to apply for a scholarship as a refugee student in the UK?
Refugee students need to prepare academic certificates, language proficiency test results, proof of refugee status, and other supporting documents.

Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
Yes, refugee students can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time, but they must meet the eligibility criteria and requirements for each scholarship.

How long does it take to receive a scholarship decision in the UK?
The scholarship decision timeline varies depending on the scholarship and university, but refugee students can expect to receive a decision within 2-6 months after submitting their application.

Can I bring my family with me to the UK if I receive a scholarship?
Refugee students who receive a scholarship may be eligible to bring their family members to the UK, but they must meet the eligibility criteria and requirements for family reunion.

How do I apply for university admission as a refugee student in the UK?
Refugee students should research and select eligible universities, prepare required documents, and submit applications for admission to the desired university and program.

For more information about refugee support and scholarships, please visit the [UK Government website](https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/home-office) and the [Universities UK website](https://www.universitiesuk.ac.uk/).

## Conclusion and Next Steps
Fully funded scholarships in the UK provide an opportunity for refugee students to pursue higher education and build a better future for themselves. By following the application steps, preparing required documents, and meeting the eligibility criteria, refugee students can increase their chances of receiving a scholarship. For more information about scholarships and refugee support, please visit the [MigrantScholar blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog).
